title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR DRIED MEAT Africa

sections.section: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR DRIED MEAT Africa
sections.summary: Scope: the standard applies to dried meat from cattle, pigs, camel, sheep, goat, poultry, donkey, horse and game intended for direct human consumption or for further processing.
Document identifier CXS 350R-2022, adopted in 2022 (Regional Standard for Dried Meat, Africa).

Overall composition and quality requirements: raw meat must be from a single animal source and safe for consumption with animals passing ante- and post-mortem inspection by a competent authority; product quality must meet specified physical/chemical limits including moisture, water activity, fat, protein and ash.

Product description: dried meat are dried, cured and/or smoked meats free from surface fat, bones, cartilage and tendons, typically cut into pieces, optionally salted or spiced, hygienically prepared and uniformly cured/dried/cooked/smoked; edible offal is excluded.

Essential composition and quality factors require use of single-animal-source raw meat that has passed ante- and post-mortem inspection and optional use of curing agents; the standard sets physical/chemical limits (moisture ≤35%, water activity ≤0.85, crude fat ≤25%, crude protein ≥20%, ash ≤8%, and edible salt per GMP).
Raw meat essential ingredient: must be from a single animal source, safe for human consumption, and obtained from animals that passed ante-mortem and post-mortem inspection by a competent authority.
Raw meat requirement: product must be made from a single type of meat characteristic of that species and sourced from animals inspected ante- and post-mortem by a competent authority.
Optional ingredients permitted include curing agents such as food-grade salt, spices and condiments, and edible oil.
Essential quality factors: ingredients must be of good quality and fit for human consumption and conform to relevant Codex Alimentarius standards where applicable.

Quality factors table: dried meat must meet physical/chemical limits—moisture ≤35%, water activity ≤0.85, crude fat ≤25%, crude protein ≥20%, ash ≤8%—and edible salt should follow good manufacturing practices.

Food additives: antioxidants and preservatives are acceptable if used according to the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for food category 08.2, and flavourings must comply with the Guidelines for the Use of Flavourings (CXG 66-2008).

Contaminants: products must comply with maximum levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides and veterinary drugs.

Hygiene: preparation and handling are recommended to follow the General Principles of Food Hygiene and related Codex codes/guidelines (including microbiological criteria, meat hygiene codes, low‑moisture foods guidance and pathogen control guidelines) and applicable slaughter and process validation guidance.

Packaging and labelling: labelling must follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) with product name specifying meat type, ingredient declaration, and best-before date (non-retail containers per CXS 346-2021); packaging must be clean, hygienic, moisture‑proof, well‑sealed food‑grade materials that do not adversely affect the product and may be vacuum packaged.

Labelling (detailed): pre-packaged dried meat must be labelled per CXS 1-1985, including the product name stating the type of meat (and local/common name), ingredient list declaring meat type, and best-before date; non-retail container labelling must follow CXS 346-2021.

sections.section: 7.1.1 The products covered by the provisions of this  standard shall be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985). 12  In addition, the following specific provisions apply:
sections.summary: Specific labelling requirements: the product name must state the type of meat (and local/common name), the ingredient list must declare the type of meat used, and a best-before date must be provided, with any additional ingredient-specific labelling complying with relevant codes and standards.
Non-retail container labelling must comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Packaging requirement: dried meat shall be packed in clean, hygienic, moisture‑proof, well‑sealed food‑grade containers made of materials that do not adversely affect the product, and may be vacuum packaged.
Section heading introducing methods of analysis and sampling and referencing the detailed methods to be used for compliance assessment.

Obligation that, to check compliance with this standard, methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the standard’s provisions shall be used.
